target.name: Zeta Surgical
target.founder: Jose Amich, Raahil Sha, William Gormley

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $100M last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 5 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Biotech & Health. Range: $600M - $3.3B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Biotech & Health P/S median is 7.8x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health

data.narrative:
Zeta Surgical is a Series A-stage biotech company seeking valuation after raising $23M to date. The current post-money valuation stands at $100M. The comparable universe consists of 3 public companies (RXRX, TEM, ONT.L) and 8 private peers in Biotech & Health, including later-stage companies such as Eikon Therapeutics, Neko Health, New Limit, Asimov, and Profluent.

Two valuation methods are applicable. Stage-progression analysis, using a typical 2.0x step-up multiplier from the $100M Series A post-money valuation, implies a $200M valuation. Peer-median analysis across five same-sector and adjacent-stage comparables yields a median of $810M, with a range spanning $600M to $3.3B. Public-comp anchoring cannot be applied due to the absence of revenue data for the target company, which is standard for undisclosed private companies.

Triangulating the two applicable methods produces a valuation range of $170M (low) to $931M (high), with a median of $505M. The wide spread reflects tension between the stage-progression method, which anchors to the current $100M valuation, and peer-median analysis, which reflects the substantially higher valuations of Series B biotech companies. Key uncertainties include whether later-stage peer valuations appropriately reflect Zeta's current stage and whether the company's progress warrants step-up multiples at the higher end of the peer range.
